skill.name: remotion-render
skill.description: Render videos from React/Remotion component code via inference.sh. Pass TSX code, get MP4. Supports all Remotion APIs: useCurrentFrame, useVideoConfig, spring, interpolate, AbsoluteFill, Sequence. Configurable resolution, FPS, duration, codec. Use for: programmatic video generation, animated graphics, motion design, data-driven videos, React animations to video. Triggers: remotion, render video from code, tsx to video, react video, programmatic video, remotion render, code to video, animated video, motion graphics code, react animation video
skill.summary: Render React/Remotion TSX components to MP4 videos via the inference.sh belt CLI. Configurable resolution, FPS, duration, and codec.
skill.author: halt-catch-fire

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 32 external_command instances and 6 hardcoded URLs plus 3 weak-crypto matches, but every finding is a false positive. The file is pure markdown documentation: the 'shell backticks' are JSON strings inside fenced bash code blocks, the URLs all point to legitimate inference.sh and remotion.dev docs, and the crypto matches are triggered by the words 'codec' and 'Required' in plain prose. No code is executed by the skill itself; rendering happens remotely on inference.sh infrastructure.

content.user_title: Render React Code to MP4 Video
content.value_statement: Producing motion graphics from React code normally requires a full Remotion toolchain. This skill sends your TSX component to inference.sh and returns a rendered MP4 with configurable resolution, FPS, and codec.
actual_capabilities: Renders React/Remotion TSX components to MP4 video files via the inference.sh belt CLI
Supports core Remotion APIs including useCurrentFrame, useVideoConfig, spring, interpolate, AbsoluteFill, and Sequence
Configures width, height, fps, duration_seconds, codec, and component props per render
Provides both a belt CLI interface and a Python SDK with streaming progress updates
Imports from remotion and react, including helpers like Audio, Video, and Img
limitations: Requires the belt CLI from inference.sh to be installed locally
Requires authentication via 'belt login' before any render will run
Rendering happens on inference.sh infrastructure, so an internet connection is required
Only Remotion-compatible React components can be rendered; arbitrary JavaScript outside that surface is not supported

use_cases.title: Generate Product Demo Videos
use_cases.description: Create short animated product walkthroughs from React components without setting up a video editor.

use_cases.title: Build Animated Data Visualizations
use_cases.description: Turn datasets into animated counters, charts, and progress indicators exported as MP4 clips.

use_cases.title: Produce Branded Social Media Clips
use_cases.description: Render square or vertical motion-graphics intros, outros, and announcements from reusable TSX components.

prompt_templates.title: Hello World Render
prompt_templates.prompt: Render a 3-second Hello World animation in 1920x1080 at 30fps. Use a black background with white text that fades from 0 to full opacity over the first second.

prompt_templates.title: Fade-In Welcome Screen
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 2-second 1920x1080 video at 30fps that fades in the word 'Welcome' from opacity 0 to 1 over 30 frames on a dark navy background.

prompt_templates.title: Animated Progress Counter
prompt_templates.prompt: Render a 5-second 1080x1080 square video at 60fps showing a percentage counter that animates from 0 to 100 over the full duration, with a 'Loading' label below the counter.

prompt_templates.title: Spring Scale Animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Render a 2-second 1080x1080 video at 60fps of a white rounded square that scales up from 0 using a Remotion spring with damping 10 and stiffness 100 on a purple background.

output_examples.input: Render a 3-second 1920x1080 video at 30fps with a fading Hello World title on black background
output: Stored on inference.sh and returned as a downloadable URL
Title fades from invisible to fully opaque over the first second

output_examples.input: Create a 5-second progress counter animation in 1080x1080 at 60fps
output: Square MP4 with a 0% to 100% progress counter
Loading label rendered below the counter
Suitable for Instagram or LinkedIn feed posts

best_practices: Use AbsoluteFill as the root container so the component fills the configured width and height
Set duration_seconds, width, height, and fps explicitly on every render call for predictable output
Use the interpolate and spring helpers from Remotion for smooth frame-based animations instead of setTimeout or setInterval
anti_patterns: Do not write to the local filesystem from inside the TSX component, because the render runs remotely on inference.sh
Do not perform network requests inside the component code, since the sandbox is the inference.sh render environment
Do not omit the default export from the TSX component, or the render will fail to find the entry point

faq.question: Do I need to install Remotion locally?
faq.answer: No. Rendering happens remotely on inference.sh. You only need the belt CLI installed and logged in.

faq.question: What inputs does the render app accept?
faq.answer: A TSX code string, an optional composition ID, props, width, height, fps, duration in seconds, and a codec name.

faq.question: Can I stream render progress?
faq.answer: Yes. The Python SDK supports streaming and yields progress percentages plus the final video URL.

faq.question: Which Remotion APIs are supported?
faq.answer: useCurrentFrame, useVideoConfig, spring, interpolate, AbsoluteFill, Sequence, Audio, Video, and Img.

faq.question: Do I need an inference.sh account?
faq.answer: Yes. Run 'belt login' once to authenticate, then subsequent renders use that session.

faq.question: What video formats can I render?
faq.answer: MP4 output with a configurable codec. Width, height, FPS, and duration are all configurable per render call.
